name: createApp()
description: Returns an application instance with Express-like methods: use(), get(), post(), put(), delete(), and listen().

name: json([opts])
description: Parses JSON request bodies and populates req.body.

options.notes: Maximum body size (bytes or unit string like '1mb').
Only accepts objects and arrays when true.
Optional JSON reviver passed to JSON.parse.
Mime type matcher for the parser.
When true allows rich objects; when false returns simple key/value pairs.
Maximum body size.
Mime type matcher.

name: text([opts])
description: Reads plain text request bodies into req.body.

options.notes: Mime matcher for which requests are treated as text.
Maximum body size.
Character encoding used to decode the incoming bytes.

name: raw([opts])
description: Receives raw bytes as a Buffer on req.body.
example: app.post('/upload', raw(), (req, res) => { /* req.body is a Buffer */ })

options.notes: Mime matcher for the raw parser.

name: multipart({ dir, maxFileSize })
description: Streaming multipart parser that writes incoming file parts to disk and exposes req.body.files and req.body.fields.

options.notes: Directory to stream uploaded parts to (absolute or relative).
Maximum size in bytes for a single uploaded file.

name: static(rootPath, opts)
description: Serves files from a filesystem folder with Content-Type detection and optional caching.

options.notes: Filename to serve for directory requests, or false to disable.
How to treat dotfiles in served directories.
Cache-Control max-age value for served files (e.g., '1h').
Hook to set custom response headers per file (res, filePath) => void.

name: cors([opts])
description: Small CORS middleware used in the demo. Typical options to control allowed origins, methods, and credentials.

options.notes: Allowed origins. Can be '*' (any), an array of origins, or a function for dynamic checking.
Allowed HTTP methods.
Allowed request headers.
Whether to allow credentials (cookies, Authorization header).

name: fetch(url, opts)
description: Small Node HTTP client returning an object with status, headers and helpers: text(), json(), arrayBuffer(). Supports timeout and progress callbacks.

options.notes: HTTP method to use.
Request headers.
Request body for non-GET methods.
Request timeout in ms.
Callback receiving { loaded, total } during download.
Callback receiving { loaded, total } during upload (when supported).
